24.reason: [The loop condition may cause out-of-bounds access when j becomes negative. Also, the logic for connecting segments is flawed because it only checks backward and assumes sorted order by end_x, but connection should be based on matching endpoints regardless of x-order beyond adjacency.]

56.reason: [The comparison function returns difference which may cause integer overflow; more importantly, sorting only by end_x is insufficient鈥攕egments should be ordered to allow proper chaining, typically by start_x or in topological order of connections. This leads to incorrect DP propagation in the nested loops.]
